claim: Google Meet can create and distribute AI meeting notes through Docs and Calendar for eligible subscriptions.
Teams Facilitator provides real-time notes and preview task-management capabilities for eligible scheduled meetings.
decision_impact: Native meeting agents cover more than summaries.

claim: Zoom includes core AI Companion capabilities with eligible paid Zoom Workplace services.
Zoom AI Companion can join eligible Google Meet and Microsoft Teams meetings as a participant.
decision_impact: Cross-platform access is no longer independent-tool-only.

claim: Fireflies supports both calendar-driven bot capture and system-audio capture with different feature availability.
decision_impact: Capture mode changes product value and trust tradeoffs.
notes: Relative page date converted using 2026-07-19 access date.

claim: Notion requires consent confirmation and exposes audio permission retention and workspace-control considerations.
Zoom templates and Asana workflows support formatting and creating work from meeting content.
decision_impact: Task generation alone is covered by incumbent workflows.
counterevidence: Creation does not prove correct ownership or completion.
notes: Asana workflow is a second direct source in the report.

claim: The more promising gap to test is commitment verification plus evidence and governance rather than generic summarization.
counterevidence: Specialized capture may still win on quality deployment or vertical workflow.
notes: Derived from C1-C7; direct source is representative rather than sole premise.

claim: Bot native and system-audio capture have materially different visibility permission and feature tradeoffs.
counterevidence: Users may prefer one global default.
notes: Derived also from C2 C4 and C6.

claim: A public user report indicates configuration and output review can be a point of friction.
decision_impact: Do not assume automatic output needs no review.
counterevidence: One report is not prevalence evidence.

claim: Evidence-linked human confirmation of candidate commitments is the highest-priority product hypothesis.
decision_impact: Run a direct-draft versus confirm-first experiment.
counterevidence: Review time may eliminate the benefit.
notes: Paper is lecture note-taking not meetings; product premises are C2 C7 C10.

claim: Public sources do not establish adoption productivity or willingness to pay for any proposed opportunity.
counterevidence: Future telemetry or field study could overturn this.
